title: ID Agents Control Center Wiki
description: Human-readable wiki and editable UI schematic for the Control Center. Each page.body field is the prose wiki article; the desktop app still reads page nav metadata for sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility, and keeps the structured sections as supporting implementation detail. README and product-spec setup guidance now tell first-run operators to verify Settings manager-extension readiness before relying on full Control Center features.

editContract.hotReload: The desktop app polls this file while running. Local edits to body prose, nav labels, icons, order, visibility, page descriptions, tabs, tiles, fields, dropdowns, controls, displays, and routing notes appear in the UI without a rebuild.
editContract.pathOverride: Set IDCTL_WIKI_PATH=/absolute/path/to/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json to drive the UI from a different local schematic file.
editContract.routeRule: A page only becomes navigable if its route is implemented in idctl-desktop/src/renderer/App.tsx. Unknown routes are shown in the Wiki but skipped by the sidebar.
editContract.body: pages[].body is markdown prose for operators. Keep each article grounded in current renderer behavior, explain when to use the page, and call out routing or safety boundaries. Do not remove nav, route, component, sourceFiles, or structured sections because those continue to drive the live UI and schematic detail.
editContract.releaseDrift: scripts/check-wiki.mjs compares this appVersion and updated marker with idctl-desktop/package.json, validates page sourceFiles, and fails when a page source file changes without docs/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json changing in the same worktree diff. Set IDCTL_WIKI_DRIFT_BASE=<ref> to compare committed branch changes against a base ref as well.
editContract.releaseNaming: Operator-facing release notes, ops/deployer handoffs, and ops-team content-ops release-cycle wiki updates should say IDACC release / idacc release, not IACC release / iacc release. IDACC means ID Agent Control Center.

Renderer mutations also emit coarse sync-domain invalidations so mounted Dashboard, Work, Inbox, Projects, and Health views refresh together after plans, goals, loops, schedules, tasks, project, org, and agent-capability changes. Settings defaults the manual backend form to Ollama, exposes a first-run readiness checkpoint for manager connection, Control Center manager-extension /capabilities feature and route compatibility, a copyable manager compatibility report, provider routing, local runtime, synced backends, and guarded setup actions, stamps provider Connect & sync through the bridge before model-list writes, re-probes local discovery rows before Add/Add all writes, shows scan age before local routing decisions, starts Local LLM stacks on the start-here filter, shows stack setup notes inline, separates configured backend/running scan evidence from package install authority, removes inferred stack uninstall actions, and demotes guided/advanced stack commands to review-first actions; it also shows coordinator status as read-only with an HR Manager routing handoff, while non-embedded Chat keeps its coordinator marker read-only and Health lead markers hand routing edits to HR Manager. pages.body:
# Dashboard

Dashboard is the daily command view. It combines lead chat for the selected team with all-team coordination, active task state, recent events, and hierarchy context so an operator can steer work without losing fleet awareness.

## Use it for

- Send a request to the selected team's lead while watching coordination across every running team.
- Check whether work is moving through the leadership chain before opening the deeper Work or Health pages.
- Read the event stream when you need fast context on what just changed.

## Current guardrails

Dashboard is now observe-first by default. Command-palette project and org sync entries open Projects or HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync instead of mutating tracker or hierarchy state immediately. The shortcuts drawer shows Probe all, Refresh, and owner-page handoffs only; project sync and org sync no longer have duplicate direct Dashboard buttons, so tracker and hierarchy writes stay on their owner pages. Primary fleet-lead changes are no longer exposed as a Dashboard selector; use HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync for the routing diff preview.

## Watch for

The Dashboard is not the fleet roster grid. Agent lifecycle controls, probes, and full roster inspection live on Health through AgentTable. If the question is "who is running?" use Health; if the question is "what should the lead do next?" start here.

pages.body:
# Chat

Chat is the full conversation workspace behind Dashboard lead chat. It owns sessions, target selection, project focus, attachments, generated images, live traces, resumable polling, unread state, and saved history.

## Use it for

- Continue or inspect a conversation with complete message history.
- Attach files, paste context, generate image context, or focus the dispatch on a tracked project.
- Review the behind-the-scenes trace for tool, file, and delegation activity after a reply lands.

## Routing note

Chat is currently embedded into Dashboard and hidden from normal sidebar navigation. Dashboard uses the same dispatch path but locks the target to the selected team's coordinator; direct Chat use is for contextual or deep-link flows that need the full session surface. Non-embedded Chat can choose the message target, but its coordinator star is read-only. Use HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync for coordinator and primary-lead changes.

pages.body:
# Inbox

Inbox is the operator decision queue. It separates direct blocker questions and manager inbox items from the general event feed so work that needs a human answer is visible and actionable.

## Use it for

- Answer option questions surfaced by agents or Work blocker scans.
- Reply to manager inbox items that are explicitly waiting on the operator.
- Dismiss stale prompts after deciding they no longer need a response.

## Operating rule

Use Inbox to resolve blockers, then return to Chat or Work for continued execution. It is intentionally narrower than a conversation view: the goal is to clear pending decisions and update the linked task state where the manager provides a task reference.

pages.body:
# Work

Work is the execution surface for plans, task cards, schedules, loops, and dream review. It turns operator intent into tracked manager work and gives the team a place to move items through planning, assignment, review, and completion.

## Start here when

- A request needs to become tasks, not just a chat reply.
- You need to triage unassigned work, assign a fleet objective, or surface blockers for a human decision.
- You want scheduled follow-up, recurring loops, or reviewed idea drafts.

## Tab guide

- Goals: objective capture, status, AI refinement, and guarded Autopilot.
- Plans: structured plans and dispatch handoff.
- Tasks: Kanban execution, review lanes, scoped assignment, and blocker surfacing.
- Schedule: team-aware heartbeats and supervision reminders.
- Loops: recurring objectives.
- Dream: proposed ideas or simulations that require review before action.

Dragging cards now fresh-reads the task, lane overlay, and dependency overlay before it writes a review lane or mapped manager status. Assignment and re-dispatch also fresh-read live status, owner, and team before overwriting ownership or sending another background request. Plans now fresh-read live brain-plan title/status/mtime before status writes, blocker questions, or dispatch, and draft-plan update/promote/delete paths block if the opened draft changed. Plan dispatch resolves the primary lead by team/name, hands remaining objectives directly to team leads, and keeps default/coder plus default/researcher as return-path validators rather than execution routers. Goals now fresh-read the opened goal before field edits, AI refine saves, and delete; the goal driver merges metadata into the latest saved goal, activity-increasing driver changes preview the global active Autopilot goals in scope, and driver change/run prompts recheck config plus active goals after confirmation before saving or spawning work. Loops now keep a baseline for saved chains and block stale save, run, schedule, or delete actions before overwriting a newer chain. Schedule now fresh-reads heartbeat and check-in rows before update, pause, remove, close, or bulk cleanup. Dream prevents duplicate nightly schedules for the same team/agent and fresh-checks reports before delete. Create Work now rechecks proposed plan owners, direct assignment targets, and fan-out team leads against the live all-team roster around confirmation; interval Schedule, Loop, and Dream shortcuts disclose any existing heartbeat route for the target and block if that route changes during confirmation. Assignment can dispatch to one team, selected teams, team leads, or every active agent, grouping manager plan creation by target team so owners stay scoped correctly. Schedule heartbeats use team-qualified targets, so cross-team agents and manager-level heartbeats remain visible and editable from the Schedule tab.

pages.body:
# Projects

Projects is the local workspace registry. It tracks folders and GitHub-backed repositories that agents can use as context, along with metadata that makes future dispatches safer and easier to aim.

## Use it for

- Add or import repositories before asking agents to work on them.
- Set descriptions, tags, branch context, and automation preferences that explain how a workspace should be handled.
- Review git status and prepare operator-approved git actions without turning the Control Center into an invisible committer.

## Current guardrails

Workspace sync is explicit, additive, and reviewed before it writes: Sync workspace previews the root, discovered folder count, already-tracked count, add/adopt counts, projected tracker total, and sample names, then re-runs the preview after confirmation and blocks if the scan changed. Pull and commit/push now show a review prompt with folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, untracked files, and the commit subject before changing files or pushing. Commit exits early when the preview sees no working changes. Project metadata save, status, delete, duplicate combine, repo create/link metadata, git actions, open-folder, and auto-commit mode changes fresh-read the tracker row before writing or using its path. Duplicate combine recomputes the duplicate groups from a fresh list and rechecks before save/remove. Auto-commit remains opt-in per project, throttled, and safe-state only: project cards show the current mode as a state chip, and mode changes live behind an Automation disclosure. Background checkpoints run only when the project row is unchanged, the repo has a linked remote, known compare state, no upstream-gone branch, zero ahead commits, zero behind commits, and working changes. Otherwise the checkpoint is skipped and the operator can review/request the commit manually.

## Good practice

Choose or update the project before sending code work through Chat or Work. A clear project focus reduces ambiguity for agents and makes attachments, uploads, and generated plans land in the expected workspace.

# Health

Health is the live fleet and usage view. It shows running state, token and model usage, provider activity, optional Headroom pilot readiness/policy, and the full agent roster through AgentTable.

## Use it for

- Confirm which agents are running, stopped, busy, or missing from the active fleet.
- Probe agent liveness, start or stop workers, rebuild where supported, and inspect team membership.
- Compare current token usage and model/runtime behavior before changing staffing or routing.
- Check active runtime credential-lane cooldowns and selected-agent rate-limit/failover state before assuming a model or lane is healthy.
- Check whether the optional local Headroom CLI/proxy is ready before choosing a pilot route.
- Draft pilot guardrails before a canary: Enable pilot route, Sampling, Workspace state, and the compact Trust & routing summary. Raw route/proxy/state path, Safety mode, protected categories, and validation gates live under Advanced.

## Current guardrails

Health no longer changes team coordinators directly. Lead stars are read-only markers sourced from the coordinator hierarchy, and the roster provides an Open HR Route handoff that lands on HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync for coordinator and primary routing edits. Probe all now fresh-reads the all-team running roster, shows the exact team/agent count and sample targets, rechecks that same target set after confirmation, and probes each current running agent in its own team; stopped agents are skipped. Start, reset session, stop, rebuild, and delete actions fresh-read the all-team fleet row before the review prompt and again immediately after confirmation before they mutate the manager; row-level probe fresh-reads before it runs. These actions show current status plus before/after or effect summaries where the action changes process state. Runtime, model, effort, and speed dropdowns stage local draft changes instead of writing immediately; Apply changes reviews the full diff, verifies the live row still matches the staged baseline, rechecks after the confirmation prompt, blocks stale rows, and rebuilds each touched agent once. Start/Rebuild is blocked while that agent has staged config so old saved config cannot launch underneath the draft. Runtime credential-lane cooldowns are read from the manager as observation-only state: the roster shows an active cooldown count, warns on affected rows, and the detail panel exposes the selected agent's lane, cooldown/rate-limit, and failover metadata without offering a stale override button. The Headroom pilot card stages route, sampling, state, state-root, and safety changes locally; Apply policy fresh-reads the current pilot policy before showing the diff and again after confirmation before writing local pilot policy, and Refresh asks before discarding unsaved edits.

## Boundary

Health is optimized for operational status from the store snapshot and all-team aggregate poll. It is the current home for the roster grid that older specs placed on Dashboard, but it is not a long-term analytics warehouse or routing editor. Runtime cooldown/failover state is visibility only; lane rotation remains manager-owned. Coordinator and primary routing edits belong in HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync. The Headroom card stores local pilot policy only; it does not install Headroom, start a proxy, attach MCP servers, wrap agents, or promote compressed summaries into Brain facts.

# Identity & Keys

Identity & Keys is the review surface for agent identity, wallet posture, Safe-style account state, scoped session keys, and Brain accountable-controller sync. It helps operators understand who can act and what account or key material is associated with that action.

## Use it for

- Select an agent from the all-team roster and inspect identity status, account details, session keys, and warnings.
- Confirm wallet-bearing agents are visible even when they belong to a team other than the manager's active team.
- Review delegated capability posture before relying on an agent for identity-sensitive work.
- Check whether Brain /controllers has an accountable controller link for the selected agent before trusting Brain Agents controller fallback copy.
- Keep onchain identity, wallet controller address, Safe account, and session-key concepts visible in one place.

## Wallet source

The page treats a valid controller wallet address as active identity evidence when it appears in OWS address metadata, SkillMesh address metadata, or an address-form OWS wallet field. Register and wallet-provision actions are routed through the selected agent's team so cross-team agents are resolved against the correct manager scope.

## Current guardrails

Renderer selection, proof state, backend key-provider account IDs, Safe/session actions, and the Tauri local mock key store are keyed by team/name. A controller challenge also includes the team in the signed message and cache key, so same-named agents in different teams cannot share a privileged proof. New challenge and verify signature fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before acting. Provision wallet, register identity, create account, deploy account, issue session key, and revoke session key fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before the review prompt and again after confirmation before writing. Create account, register, deploy, issue, and revoke also recheck the controller proof after confirmation; create/deploy/issue compare account state stamps, and revoke compares the exact active session row before mutating key state. The Electron bridge and Tauri fallback both require controller proof for keys:ensure, so the visible Create account button is not the only guard. The UI only issues finite, spend-capped session keys and blocks full, uncapped, or non-expiring grants. When old bare-name mock accounts or sessions match current agents, the page now shows a Legacy Authority Review card instead of silently promoting or hiding them; scoped-agent selection and copy helpers guide a manual recreation path without trusting the old record.

Brain Controller Sync reads Brain /controllers through a typed, read-only bridge. It counts active controller links, compares those links with current IDACC agents, treats team/id or team/name links as strong, treats bare agent-name links as review-only when duplicate names exist, and never creates, links, revokes, or promotes Brain controller records.

## Safety note

Existing bare-name mock accounts are not automatically promoted into team-scoped accounts. The Legacy Authority Review card can select the matching scoped agent and copy the scoped authority target, but recreate, revoke, copy, or delete policy still stays explicit. Brain /controllers evidence is also observation-only; missing or ambiguous links mean Brain Agents controller fallback should not be trusted for authority decisions. Some provider behavior still depends on the active manager backend. Treat this page as the UI contract and verify the live provider before documenting stronger guarantees about signing, custody, non-expiring keys, or production-grade authorization.

pages.body:
# HR Manager

HR Manager is the organization design and routing workspace. It covers team structure, agent creation, roster management, instructions, and outbound relay topology.

## Owner

The legal-team `hr-manager` agent owns this page's staffing workflows, instruction-drafting behavior, and optimization proposals. Escalate legal-team policy or personnel-process questions through `legal/general-counsel`.

## Tab guide

- Structure: inspect the team graph, selected agent/team context, instruction editor, rebuild, and team reassignment. Lead shortcuts open Route > Hierarchy & sync instead of writing coordinator or primary state directly.
- Build: draft and create agent rosters from blank text, recommended blueprints, manager templates, or saved configs.
- Manage: switch between Team ops for start/stop/probe/rebuild/delete and Instructions for persistent coordination directives.
- Route: switch between Overview, Relay policy, and Hierarchy & sync so relay edits, coordinator changes, and org sync are not stacked in one long pane.

## Current guardrails

Coordinator, primary lead, team relay, and per-agent relay writes fresh-read the relevant roster, hierarchy, or policy row before showing concrete before/after routing diffs. Primary lead promotion is locked to the default team; non-default teams can still set coordinators, but Structure no longer directly writes coordinator or primary state. Its lead controls open Route > Hierarchy & sync, where the guarded preview path lives. Route > Relay policy no longer exposes team reassignment, so relay editing cannot accidentally move an agent between teams. The default team's outbound relay cannot be saved as Blocked/no teams, and `default/lead`, `default/coder`, and `default/researcher` cannot receive per-agent Blocked/no-teams overrides; they can still use permissive, all-team, or selected-team relay settings. Instruction Save & rebuild fresh-reads the selected agent and the current saved instruction text before writing, so a stale editor cannot overwrite a newer directive or rebuild the wrong moved agent. Selected-agent rebuild and move controls re-resolve the current team/agent row before acting, and empty-team delete rechecks that the team still exists and still has no agents.

Team ops now uses one consistent current-state confirmation for Probe, Start, Stop, and Rebuild: it requires a verified current roster, shows running/total counts plus sample targets, blocks missing or empty teams, and rechecks the exact roster stamp after confirmation before calling the manager route. Team Builder fresh-reads the target team roster before onboarding; after agents are created it rechecks hierarchy and relay stamps before automatic team-coordinator wiring or relay policy writes, always wires `default/lead` for default-team routing, and never promotes a non-default team into the fleet-primary slot. The Build tab shows recommended blueprint coverage plus default return-path readiness, marks `default/lead` as the fixed default primary, blocks post-build default relay settings that would leave no outbound path, installs a default-primary validation preset for default/lead instead of the ordinary delegate-to-teammates preset, and adds an explicit warning before non-default teams are built without default/lead, default/coder, default/researcher, and primary/coordinator routing in place. Builder coordinator presets also tell team leads to return substantial completed work through `default/coder` and `default/researcher` validation before `default/lead` treats it as final. Org sync also previews before apply; it fresh-reads org config and hierarchy, uses the same sidecar change detection as the real sync, shows scanned agents, goal files that would change, whether the brain hierarchy will be written, planned idle rebuilds under the rebuild cap, deferred rebuilds, and sample affected team/agent rows, then blocks apply if org state changed after preview. Query-busy and task-busy rebuild deferrals are team-aware where manager data includes team names, so same-named agents in different teams are less likely to block each other.

## Operating rule

Changes here affect staffing, instructions, delegation paths, and agent process state. Review generated drafts before saving, syncing, or rebuilding, and use the graph as a whole-fleet planning aid rather than a view of only the active team. Agent instruction editing is team-qualified in the picker and save path, so selecting an agent from another team loads, saves, and rebuilds that exact team/agent instead of silently falling back to the active team. Keep Team ops, Instructions, Relay policy, and Hierarchy & sync separated during daily operation so lifecycle buttons, text edits, relay writes, and org sync previews each have their own focused review surface. Owner-page handoffs from Dashboard, Health, Settings, hidden Chat, and Structure lead shortcuts use App navigation or local focus to land directly on Route > Hierarchy & sync. The shared team-coordinator instruction source now matches the default primary lead and secondary validator return path, but deployed coordinator-skill copies should be synced or rebuilt only after an explicit maintenance decision. The Build tab also ships app-side recommended blueprints for default leadership, operations, engineering, research, and onchain teams, shows present/total coverage for each, marks default/coder and default/researcher as validators only, and keeps the default validator backbone visible so a downloaded IDACC has a useful first-run path even when the manager library is empty or older.

pages.body:
# Capabilities

Capabilities manages the extension surface available to teams, agents, and the Brain skill graph: MCP servers, skills, and plugins. It answers whether the fleet has the tools and instructions needed for a request before work is assigned.

## Use it for

- Inspect installed MCP servers, skills, bundled plugins, and Brain skill-catalog freshness.
- Attach or review capabilities for the selected team scope.
- Sync the local SKILL.md catalog into Brain so Brain dashboard Skills and Graph views can reason over the same skill definitions.
- Open Brain Fleet, Health, Skills, Learning, Agents, or Graph from the IDACC GUI without leaving the guarded dashboard-tab launcher.
- Confirm tool availability before assigning work that depends on a connector, library-specific instruction, or specialist workflow.

## Current guardrails

MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and target rebuild now fresh-read every selected target before the confirmation prompt and again immediately before writing. The guard compares team, name, runtime, status, health, MCP metadata, and skill metadata, blocks stale or moved agents, excludes newly incompatible runtimes, and passes each resolved target's team explicitly so all-team or team-scoped actions cannot drift through the active manager team. MCP attach/detach still builds the replacement MCP list from the fresh metadata row, so a stale UI cannot erase another page's server attachment.

MCP registry Add fresh-reads the registry and treats same-name adds as an explicit replace. Catalog and custom replacement drafts now require Replace existing MCP server to be armed inline, show before/after endpoint details, and recheck the registry after confirmation before writing. MCP registry removal fresh-reads before delete, rechecks the registry after confirmation, and blocks if the entry changed. Library skill delete now requires a fresh all-team usage read before removing a skill folder, so a stale rendered Fleet count cannot delete a skill still installed on an agent. Skill creation still relies on manager-side validation and conflict rejection.

The Skills tab now shows local SKILL.md count separately from Brain's current /skills/index count, preserves Brain /skills/index route/profile/generated metadata, checks for the current catalog fields (domain facets, tag facets, reuse groups, and proposal summary), reads Brain /fleet-report for Fleet live/partial/cache/stale-contract status, reads Brain /graph/app/data for Graph neighbor/source-authority contract status, and opens Brain Fleet, Health, Skills, Learning, Agents, or Graph through a fixed main-process whitelist instead of a free-form URL. Launcher buttons inherit the active IDACC review state: stale Fleet, Skills, Agents, Graph, Core, or Learning conditions are warning-styled and ask for confirmation with the review reason before opening the Brain tab; Markdown links to known Brain tabs use the same guarded opener instead of bypassing Health/Learning confirmations; nested Brain dashboard nav links to Health and Learning also carry confirmations in source, with live effect after Brain restart/redeploy. Local create, delete, and manual re-categorize actions mark Brain catalog review needed until Preview & sync Brain succeeds; if Brain reports fewer skills than the local catalog, the same review strip appears without automatically mutating Brain. If Brain returns counts but is missing the searchable catalog contract fields, the Skills chip marks Brain stale and the review strip asks for Brain restart/redeploy instead of offering a misleading local sync as the only fix. Preview & sync Brain fresh-reads local SKILL.md entries, auto-tag overlays, and Brain summary before showing the additive upsert/shared-memory boundary; it rechecks both the local catalog and the Brain /skills/index summary after confirmation before sending the catalog stamp, so a Brain-side refresh during review blocks the write. The sync additively upserts local library skills into Brain's /skills/index graph and writes a shared catalog memory, but it does not delete Brain-only skill nodes or change which agents have a skill installed. Deleting a local skill therefore refreshes the shared catalog memory but intentionally leaves Brain-learned or previously synced nodes for graph review after the operator previews and applies sync. The Brain fleet chip and review strip are read-only: when /fleet-report is unavailable, cache/partial, warning-bearing, missing the current authority-label contract, or reporting counts that disagree with IDACC's live all-team store, Capabilities surfaces that review state before operators trust Brain Fleet, Health, or Agents counts. A mismatch turns the Fleet chip into a drift state with both counts, so stale Brain dashboards such as 19/38 beside IDACC 0/67 cannot look authoritative; the nested Brain source now also marks fallback:ID_TEAM snapshots as partial when manager /teams is unavailable. The Brain graph chip follows the same pattern for /graph/app/data: it checks that filtered Graph search honors neighbors=0 and that source-authority labels are present before operators trust Graph agent/entity status copy.

## Scope note

Team scoping happens inside this page rather than only through the global footer selector. Keep interop fields explicit: MCP, OpenAPI, A2A, and signed-write discovery are separate concerns and should not be collapsed into one generic capability claim.

pages.body:
# Computer Use

Computer Use is the supervised local desktop-control broker. It shows the live display stream, permission state, pending approval prompts, and recent computer-use activity for agents that are allowed to request screen or input actions.

## Use it for

- Bless capable agents, arm or disarm control, and supervise the live stage.
- Approve or reject pending screen/input actions.
- Use pause, disarm, or panic controls quickly when local control should stop.

## Current guardrails

Blessing is scoped to the active team. The attached MCP config receives a team/name authority, the broker token map stores that scoped authority, and the arm-time bless list checks the same value before allowing screenshots or input. Old bare-name tokens are blocked by scoped arming and appear as a Legacy authority review notice near Who can drive, so operators can see stale access artifacts without automatically reauthorizing them. Review helpers can copy the scoped target and select the current agent for Bless, leaving the old token blocked.

Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode changes, and approval decisions now fresh-read broker or roster state before continuing. Arm previews the attached-agent list before and after confirmation, then asks the main process to derive the actual arm list from the current attached agents for that team. The main process compares the reviewed attached-agent stamp, preserves other teams' armed authorities, and refuses stale renderer-provided blessed lists. Background armed-list refreshes after Bless or Remove send the same attached-agent stamp, so a stale manager read cannot silently rewrite the broker's live blessed set. Empty Arm for live-view permission testing now requires checking Start live view with no blessed agents before the Arm button enables. The Bless picker resets on team change and disables submission unless the selected agent is still eligible and unblessed. Bless fresh-reads the selected agent row and MCP metadata before and after confirmation, blocks moved or changed targets, and the bridge only attaches when the current roster row still matches the reviewed agent id and name. Remove re-reads the attached row before and after confirmation, detaches only the current scoped authority, the bridge refuses same-name replacement rows, rebuilds only when that exact agent id still exists in the current roster, and refreshes the armed list if needed; stale authority for a moved or deleted agent is removed without rebuilding a same-named replacement. Allow/Deny re-reads the pending request and blocks stale or expired approval prompts. Disabling supervised per-action approval fresh-reads broker mode and still requires confirmation.

Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths and are not blocked by long attach/rebuild work. PANIC still disarms, clears pending approvals, releases held input, and records the stop in the broker audit.

## Safety boundary

Actions should run only through the armed broker and approval flow. The page depends on local OS permissions such as Screen Recording and Accessibility. Navigating away stops the live renderer view, although global notifications can still report relevant broker state. Conservative default: Arm with no blessed agents is still available for live-view or permission testing only after the empty-session review is armed; a stricter operator policy could require at least one blessed agent before arming.

pages.body:
# Settings

Settings contains local desktop configuration for hardware, manager connection, coordinator status, self-update, subscriptions, Ollama, image services, local stacks, EVM data RPCs, and inference backends.

## Use it for

- Fix manager connectivity or confirm the manager URL used by the app.
- Confirm the connected manager exposes the Control Center extension contract before relying on IDACC-only pages.
- Copy a manager compatibility report when a downloaded install is pointed at a stock, stale, or partially upgraded manager.
- Review subscription CLI state, local model providers, image generation services, EVM data endpoints, and inference defaults.
- Bring a new install into a driveable local path: download starter model, add Ollama backend, sync models, and make the local backend default when desired.
- Check for, stage, and relaunch into app updates.

## Current guardrails

Coordinator is now read-only on Settings. The Connection card shows the active team's current coordinator and hands routing changes to HR Manager, where coordinator and primary-lead writes run through hierarchy fresh-reads and before/after previews. The first-run readiness strip now reads manager:capabilities and marks the install as needing a manager update when /capabilities is missing, the Control Center API version is absent, or required extension features or route signatures are not present; Re-check manager repeats that read without changing manager state, and Copy manager report places the manager URL, connection state, required/reported API versions, extension id, feature coverage, and missing route signatures on the clipboard. Provider default, enable/disable, remove, connect/sync, same-name add/replace, local-discovery add, and Add all new now fresh-read the current provider list before writing. Connect & sync also sends the reviewed provider stamp into the bridge, and the bridge rechecks that stamp after the live probe before recording the model-list cache. Default promotion is blocked unless the backend is enabled, key-ready, and route-ready with a live, synced, or preset model list; the table disables stale default stars and the Local models strip only lists local providers that are actually route-ready. Disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked before and after confirmation. Same-name provider replacement now requires an inline Replace existing backend arm, shows before/after endpoint details, and rechecks the provider after confirmation; discovered-server Add and Add all re-run discovery, compare the current server snapshot, show scan age, and de-dupe against the fresh provider list before writing any backend.

EVM RPC save, replace, remove, and check fresh-read the current endpoint row before acting; replacing an existing endpoint shows before/after URLs. Local Ollama auto-backend creation rechecks current providers before adding the default Ollama backend. Local concurrency Apply fresh-reads the manager's current local inference limit before writing, and image-server Save/Clear fresh-reads the current preferred local image generator before changing local-vs-cloud routing. Local LLM stacks now open on the start-here filter, order install-ease tags as start here, easy, guided, advanced, and expert, show each setup note inline, keep starter/easy paths as primary Install actions, and make guided/advanced stacks review their command before any Terminal run. The stack catalog labels running entries as scan snapshots, labels matching configured endpoints as backend configured, and no longer shows package uninstall commands from inferred configured/running evidence. Terminal install commands remain reviewed and explicit, with visible clipboard fallback when automation is blocked. First-run local setup now defaults manual backend add to Ollama, starts the local model pull field on qwen3:1.7b, and exposes a top readiness checkpoint for manager extension feature/route compatibility, manager connectivity, explicit-vs-auto routing, synced local runtime readiness, ready backend counts, missing keys, and guarded quick actions for manager recheck, manager report copy, starter download, Ollama backend add, sync, explicit default, and local scan. The Local models section repeats that path beside the model catalog, and local runtime readiness now means a local backend is synced/live rather than merely installed or configured. Sign-out, stack install, model removal, and provider removal remain explicit operator actions.

## Boundary

These settings are app-local configuration and infrastructure routing, not manager task data. Coordinator/routing edits belong in HR Manager, not Settings. Local discovery rows are snapshots and must be re-probed before provider writes; stack configured/running badges are not proof that a package is installed or safe to uninstall. The manager-extension readiness tile and report are read-only compatibility evidence for whether the connected id-agents manager includes the Control Center extension feature and route contract used by the downloaded app. Because release and update behavior is surfaced here, keep this page aligned with the package version and release process; the wiki drift check exists to catch stale release-facing documentation before shipping.

pages.purpose: Current audit record for controls that can rewrite live agent, task, project, capability, identity, or provider state and therefore need review, confirmation, or stronger state synchronization.
pages.scope: Documentation and implementation record for the current modified build. It records the first full dashboard-page audit, one hundred twenty-four conservative guardrail implementation passes, and the policy decisions still needed before more disruptive UX simplification.

tabs.label: Dashboard
tabs.description: Dashboard is now observe-first by default: command-palette project/org sync entries route to owner pages, the shortcuts drawer shows observe and owner-page handoffs only, duplicate direct project/org sync shortcuts have been removed, and primary-lead changes move to HR Manager. The hidden non-embedded Chat address panel also keeps coordinator marking read-only so it cannot bypass HR Manager if surfaced later.

tabs.label: Work
tabs.description: Task status, owner, redispatch, reconcile, create-work dispatch, plan status/dispatch, goal/loop/schedule/dream writes fresh-read live state before mutating. Create Work rechecks plan owners, direct assignment targets, fan-out team leads, and heartbeat replacement routes around confirmation. Plan dispatch now resolves the team-scoped primary lead and instructs that lead to delegate to team leads directly while coder/researcher validate completed work on return.

tabs.label: Projects
tabs.description: Git actions, direct commit/push, auto-commit, repo create/link/fork, and pull actions can change repository or project-tracker state. Workspace sync now previews root/folder/add/adopt counts and rechecks the scan after confirmation before tracker writes; commit/push and pull preview branch/remote/status/diff/untracked files and recheck that git/diff stamp after confirmation, project-row writes fresh-read current tracker state, auto-commit mode changes sit behind an Automation disclosure, and background checkpoints only run when project and branch/remote state are exactly safe immediately before committing.

tabs.label: Health
tabs.description: Runtime/model/effort/speed selectors stage local drafts and apply only after full diff review plus fresh fleet-row checks before and after confirmation. Probe all previews and rechecks the all-team running roster before probing each target in its own team; Start, reset session, stop, rebuild, delete, and row probe fresh-read the current row before changing context or process state. Lead stars are read-only hierarchy markers with an HR Manager handoff for coordinator routing. Runtime credential-lane cooldowns are surfaced as read-only manager state so operators can see why a lane is cooling without overriding the manager. Headroom pilot policy edits are staged until Apply policy reviews the diff and rechecks the current policy before and after confirmation.

tabs.label: Identity & Keys
tabs.description: Controller proof gates privileged actions; renderer selection, proof cache, backend account IDs, Safe/session actions, and the Tauri local mock key store are team/name keyed. Identity actions fresh-read the selected agent row before the review prompt and again after confirmation; account/session-dependent actions also compare account or session stamps before writing. Legacy bare-name key records surface as review-only evidence instead of being auto-promoted.

tabs.label: HR Manager
tabs.description: Coordinator changes, default-only primary lead promotion, relay overrides, instruction saves with rebuild, Team Builder auto-wiring, selected-agent rebuild, team lifecycle/probe actions, empty-team delete, and org sync fresh-read current roster/config/hierarchy state before writing, probing, or rebuilding. Team ops previews exact targets and blocks if the roster changes after confirmation. Route labels stale/non-running coordinator evidence and only running agents can be selected as current routing leads. Team Builder coordinator presets include the default coder/researcher validation return path, default/lead gets a distinct primary validation preset, Manage/Route split risky controls into focused modes, the shared team-coordinator skill source teaches the same primary/secondary validation loop, and Build now has first-run recommended team blueprints.

tabs.label: Capabilities
tabs.description: MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and rebuild fresh-read selected targets before and after confirmation; registry add/remove recheck current catalogs, registry removal rechecks after confirmation, and skill delete verifies fresh all-team usage before removing records. Same-name MCP registry replacement now needs an inline replacement arm plus post-confirm registry recheck. The Skills tab now shows local-vs-Brain skill-index status, flags local create/delete/re-categorize drift, flags missing Brain /skills/index route/profile/facet/reuse/proposal fields as a stale catalog contract, reads Brain /fleet-report as a read-only fleet-authority chip, reads Brain /graph/app/data as a read-only graph-contract chip, and keeps additive Preview & sync Brain explicit.

tabs.label: Computer Use
tabs.description: Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode changes, and Allow/Deny now fresh-read broker, pending-approval, attached-agent, or roster state before continuing. Empty Arm for permission testing requires an explicit no-blessed-agent review before Arm enables. Tokens, attached-agent rows, and the arm bless-list use team/name scoped authority; attach/detach require exact current ID/name matches and armed-list refreshes use attached-list stamps. Legacy bare-name tokens surface as review-only evidence and stay blocked by scoped arming.

tabs.label: Settings
tabs.description: The first-run readiness strip now reads manager /capabilities, blocks ready state when the connected id-agents manager lacks the Control Center extension feature or route contract, and offers a copyable compatibility report instead of an automatic manager updater. Provider default/toggle/remove/connect/add, local discovery adds, EVM RPC writes, local concurrency, and image-server preference changes now fresh-read current settings before writing; provider Connect & sync is bridge-stamped before recording model lists. Same-name provider replacement needs an inline replacement arm before the final confirmation. Local discovery Add/Add all re-probe server snapshots and fresh provider lists before writing. Coordinator status is read-only here; HR Manager owns coordinator and primary routing edits. The last enabled inference backend cannot be disabled or removed. Local model readiness now distinguishes installed/configured from synced/default-ready, while the stack catalog labels configured/running evidence without offering inferred uninstall actions.

controls.label: Preview before apply
controls.description: Use for org sync, git commit/push, capability bulk apply, and hierarchy/routing changes. The preview should show target agents/teams, files or repos touched, and whether a rebuild or push will happen.

controls.label: Stage then apply
controls.description: Use for compact dropdown-heavy config surfaces such as Health runtime/model/effort/speed. Draft changes locally, review the whole diff, block stale rows, and rebuild or write once per touched target.

controls.label: Fresh task preflight
controls.description: Use for task-card actions that mutate manager status, owner, or background dispatch. Refetch the task list plus lane/dependency overlays, block when status/owner/team/lane changed, and refresh before the operator tries again.

controls.label: Fresh plan preflight
controls.description: Use for brain-plan status writes, blocker-question creation, dispatch, and draft-plan promotion or deletion. Refetch live plan title/status/mtime or draft version/updatedAt/content before applying the action, and have the main writer reject stale expected brain-plan status snapshots.

controls.label: Fresh goal preflight
controls.description: Use for saved goal edits, AI refine saves, deletion, and driver metadata writes. Refetch the goal updatedAt marker before saving from an opened editor, and merge background driver metadata onto the latest saved goal instead of an older loaded copy.

controls.label: Fresh loop preflight
controls.description: Use for saved sequential loop chains. Keep the opened loop's updatedAt baseline, block save/run/schedule/delete when the saved chain changed, refresh the editor to the latest chain, and reload the saved record after each save so the baseline stays aligned.

controls.label: Fresh schedule preflight
controls.description: Use for heartbeat, schedule, check-in, and dream-schedule controls. Refetch current schedules or check-ins before update/pause/remove/close, block stale rendered rows, disclose and recheck Create Work interval-heartbeat replacements, and reject duplicate recurring Dream schedules before creating another calendar check-in.

controls.label: Fresh project preflight
controls.description: Use for project tracker writes and filesystem/git actions. Capture the opened project row, refetch before saving status/metadata/delete/auto-commit, re-anchor git actions to the current path, and recompute duplicate groups before bulk save/remove.

controls.label: Project automation disclosure
controls.description: Use for background project automation such as auto-commit. Keep the card row read-only with a state chip, move mode changes into an explicit Automation disclosure, and retain fresh-project preflight plus safe-state checkpoint rules before any write or commit request.

controls.label: Fresh agent preflight
controls.description: Use for Health roster actions and staged agent config writes. Refetch the all-team fleet row before lifecycle, row probe, lead, delete, and Apply actions; for Probe all, refetch the all-team running target set before and after confirmation; block if name/team/status/runtime/model/effort/speed/health/port or target set changed before writing or probing.

controls.label: Fresh identity preflight
controls.description: Use for Identity & Keys privileged actions. Refetch the selected team/agent row before challenge, verify, wallet, account, deploy, and issue actions; refetch the latest account/session row before revoke so stale session state cannot disable the wrong grant.

controls.label: Fresh HR preflight
controls.description: Use for HR Manager staffing and routing writes. Refetch the target roster, hierarchy, relay policy, org config, or instruction text before coordinator/default-primary changes, relay overrides, instruction save+rebuild, team lifecycle/probe actions, Team Builder post-build team-coordinator wiring, empty-team delete, and org sync apply. For team-wide operations, stamp the reviewed roster and block if it changes after confirmation.

controls.label: Focused HR modes
controls.description: Split HR Manager Manage into Team ops and Instructions, and Route into Overview, Relay policy, and Hierarchy & sync so lifecycle buttons, instruction writes, relay edits, and org sync previews are not stacked in one surface.

controls.label: Coordinator instruction sync gate
controls.description: The central id-agents team-coordinator skill now matches the primary lead, secondary validator, parallel delegation, and goal-alignment policy. Deployed agent copies should be synced/rebuilt only after operator approval because that can touch live agent folders and processes.

controls.label: Downloadable team blueprints
controls.description: Keep first-run team creation useful even when the manager has no team library: IDACC provides safe spec-prefill blueprints for default leadership, operations, engineering, research, and onchain teams, and source changes reset stale roster drafts before parsing.

controls.label: Default primary validator preset
controls.description: Use when building or dispatching through default/lead. The primary lead delegates execution directly to team leads; default/coder and default/researcher validate completed work on the return path and judge accomplishments against the primary goal first, then secondary goals, then the objective.

controls.label: Runtime cooldown observation
controls.description: Use when the manager owns runtime credential lanes, cooldowns, rate-limit failover, or provider recovery. Fetch current cooldown state, display affected lanes/agents as read-only operational truth, and avoid adding override controls until manager-side lane policy exposes a guarded write API.

controls.label: Manager response drain
controls.description: Use for every shared manager HTTP client and desktop bridge poll surface. Bound normal and long-poll requests with explicit timeouts, coalesce duplicate in-flight read calls, and even on 4xx/5xx or graceful older-manager fallback paths, consume or cancel response bodies before throwing so Electron and TUI runtimes release sockets instead of accumulating CLOSE_WAIT or SYN_SENT connections.

controls.label: Manager keepalive budget
controls.description: Use for the id-agents manager HTTP server. Cap idle keepalive, headers wait, and requests per socket so stale or pre-fix dashboard clients cannot hold thousands of loopback sockets forever; apply through a manager rebuild/restart maintenance window.

controls.label: Fresh capability preflight
controls.description: Use for Capabilities target writes. Refetch every selected team/agent before and after confirmation, block moved or newly incompatible agents, compare current MCP/skill metadata, and pass each resolved target team explicitly before MCP, skill, or rebuild writes. For destructive catalog deletes, re-read registry state after confirmation and verify fresh all-team skill usage rather than trusting rendered counts.

controls.label: MCP registry replacement arm
controls.description: Use for same-name MCP server replacement. Detect catalog or custom drafts that collide with the rendered registry, show before/after endpoints, require Replace existing MCP server before enabling replacement, then fresh-read again after confirmation before mcp:add writes.

controls.label: Fresh Computer Use preflight
controls.description: Use for Computer Use Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode, and approval decisions. Refetch broker status, pending requests, attached agents, and selected roster rows before acting; recheck after confirmation when authority or rebuild state can change. Bridge-side attach/detach must require exact current agent id/name matches, and armed-list refreshes must include an attached-list stamp.

controls.label: Computer Use empty-arm review
controls.description: Use when Screen Recording is granted but no agents are blessed. Disable Arm until the operator explicitly acknowledges Start live view with no blessed agents; keep the broker preflight so a stale empty session cannot arm without review.

controls.label: Fresh Settings preflight
controls.description: Use for Settings provider, RPC, local concurrency, and image-server writes. Refetch the current settings row or manager value before writing, recheck after confirmation, and block stale replacement or last-enabled-backend mutations. For provider Connect & sync, carry the reviewed provider stamp through the bridge and recheck it after live probing before recording the model-list cache.

controls.label: Provider replacement arm
controls.description: Use for same-name inference backend replacement. Detect the rendered same-name provider in the add form, show before/after endpoints, require an explicit Replace existing backend arm before enabling the replace action, then still fresh-read and confirm before writing.

controls.label: Fresh read before full-list write
controls.description: Use for MCP server attach/detach and any API that posts a complete list. Refetch current agent metadata immediately before constructing the next list so stale UI state cannot erase another page's change.

controls.label: Team/id scoped authority
controls.description: Use for Identity & Keys and Computer Use so accounts, proofs, session grants, broker tokens, and blessed authority cannot collide when teams contain same-named agents. Legacy bare-name records should surface as review evidence until an explicit migration policy exists.

controls.label: Legacy authority review
controls.description: Use when older unscoped accounts, sessions, tokens, or permissions still exist. Show them as stale review artifacts, never as current authority, and require an operator policy before copying, revoking, or deleting them.

controls.label: Two-step destructive actions
controls.description: Use for reset session, rebuild active agents, provider removal, disabling the last usable backend, disabling Computer Use supervised mode, and stopping/removing recurring schedules.

controls.label: Observe-first Dashboard
controls.description: Keep Dashboard focused on observe, talk, refresh, and status. Move high-impact mutators such as primary-lead changes and org sync to HR Manager or require an explicit confirmation flow.

controls.label: Additive Brain catalog sync
controls.description: Use when an IDACC catalog feeds a Brain dashboard graph. Show the Brain count, make sync explicit, upsert current local definitions, and avoid deleting Brain-only learned nodes during refresh.

controls.label: Brain fleet authority review
controls.description: Use when IDACC links to Brain dashboard Fleet/Health/Agents status. Read /fleet-report without writing, show running/total plus live/partial/cache/stale-contract authority, compare it against IDACC's live all-team store, and keep missing authority-label or count-drift states visible until Brain is restarted, redeployed, or scoped correctly.

controls.label: Brain graph contract review
controls.description: Use when IDACC links to Brain Graph. Read /graph/app/data without writing, verify neighbors=0 support, show expanded/direct graph counts, and keep missing source-authority contracts visible until Brain is restarted or redeployed.

controls.label: Explicit Brain replay
controls.description: Use when a Brain dashboard needs compute-heavy POST analysis such as eval replay or vector comparison. Keep passive GET reports on timers, but run replay only from an explicit operator refresh and show when it has not been run in the current view.

controls.label: Same-name agent telemetry
controls.description: Use when Brain dashboards combine live all-team fleet rows with legacy name-keyed telemetry. Keep the live row visible, but mark SLA, cost, probe, or bare controller-link fallbacks ambiguous when multiple current teams share the same agent name.

routing.decisionStatus: The audit findings are surfaced. One hundred one conservative guardrail passes are implemented; remaining simplification, live Brain redeploy, manager update strategy, and legacy-authority migration should wait for operator policy.

notes: Existing protections already found: delete task, delete project, combine duplicate projects, sign out, RPC removal, model removal, reviewed stack install, team stop/rebuild, empty-team delete, Identity privileged actions, and Computer Use broker actions have confirmations or gates.
Most dangerous stale-write class found: UI computes a replacement state from a snapshot and sends a wholesale write, especially MCP server lists and git/project automation.

Most dangerous background automation found: project auto-commit can commit and push after task completion without another user click; the modified build makes enabling that behavior explicit per project.

Thirty-seventh guardrail pass stages an id-agents manager keepalive/socket budget and records the rebuild/restart boundary before it can affect the live manager.
Thirty-eighth guardrail pass moves Projects auto-commit mode changes behind an Automation disclosure while keeping the card row as a read-only state summary.
Thirty-ninth guardrail pass adds an inline Settings replacement arm before same-name inference backends can be replaced.
Fortieth guardrail pass requires an explicit Computer Use empty-arm review before starting the live view with no blessed agents.
Forty-first guardrail pass adds an inline Capabilities MCP registry replacement arm and post-confirm registry recheck before same-name server replacement.
Forty-second guardrail pass makes Capabilities local-vs-Brain skill catalog drift visible after local create/delete/re-categorize and keeps Brain sync explicit, additive, and non-destructive.

Forty-third guardrail pass stages a Brain dashboard Skills source/freshness strip that labels /skills/index, generated time, IDACC library node count, and the additive non-delete boundary before any Brain restart.
Forty-fourth guardrail pass stages Brain Fleet and Agents status-authority labels so Brain-cache fallback rows cannot masquerade as live current agent state.
Forty-fifth guardrail pass aligns active coordinator team YAML sources with the default primary/secondary validation loop while holding live /sync or rebuild propagation for operator approval.
Forty-sixth guardrail pass stages Brain Health post-prompt approval rechecks and stale queue snapshot blocking before any triage mutation.
Forty-seventh guardrail pass stages Brain Learning passive-report versus manual-replay freshness labels so old replay snapshots do not look current beside refreshed metrics.

Forty-eighth guardrail pass stages Brain Agents SLA coverage labels so unfetched telemetry cannot look like zero-cost/zero-failure health, and Brain Graph source authority labels so agent/entity statuses cannot masquerade as live lifecycle state.

Forty-ninth guardrail pass stages a read-only Brain Graph live-Fleet overlay that matches agent nodes only by internal id, scoped team/name, or unique bare name, leaving ambiguous and unmatched nodes cache-only.
Fiftieth guardrail pass makes Settings local-model setup more first-run ready by defaulting manual backend add to Ollama and surfacing model/backend/default readiness before provider changes.

Fifty-first guardrail pass makes HR Manager Build show preloaded-team coverage and default return-path readiness, promotes default/lead during default leadership builds, and confirms before non-default teams are built without the default validator backbone.

Fifty-second guardrail pass turns Capabilities Brain skill sync into a preview-and-apply flow with local catalog fresh-reads, post-confirm rechecks, and a bridge-side catalog stamp to block stale Brain writes.

Fifty-third guardrail pass adds manual legacy-authority migration aids to Identity & Keys and Computer Use: select scoped target and copy scoped authority without trusting, deleting, or auto-promoting old bare-name records.
Fifty-fourth guardrail pass lifts Settings first-run model/backend readiness into a top checkpoint with guarded quick actions and explicit auto-routing drift visibility.
Fifty-fifth guardrail pass turns the Capabilities Brain shortcut into a whitelisted six-tab Brain dashboard launcher.
Fifty-sixth guardrail pass removes the direct Settings coordinator write path and hands routing changes to HR Manager.
Fifty-seventh guardrail pass removes the hidden Chat coordinator-star write path and keeps Chat addressing target-only.
Fifty-eighth guardrail pass removes Health AgentTable coordinator writes and removes the generic renderer setCoordinator helper.
Fifty-ninth guardrail pass focuses HR routing handoffs on HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync so coordinator/org-sync review does not land on a stale generic tab.
Sixtieth guardrail pass stages a Brain Health Live Fleet Authority strip backed by /fleet-report so Health, Fleet, and Agents share the same live/partial/cache status contract.

Sixty-first guardrail pass adds an IDACC-side Brain Fleet authority chip/review strip backed by read-only /fleet-report so live counts, stale Brain service contracts, cache fallback, and partial-source warnings stay visible from the Capabilities skill catalog.

Sixty-second guardrail pass folds the updated id-agents default-leadership contract into IDACC: default/lead gets a primary-team-lead routing preset, Work plan dispatch targets the team-scoped primary, and coder/researcher stay validator-only on the return path.

Sixty-third guardrail pass adds an IDACC-side Brain Graph contract chip/review strip backed by read-only /graph/app/data so neighbor expansion support and missing source-authority labels stay visible from the Capabilities skill catalog.
Sixty-fourth guardrail pass adds an IDACC-side Brain Skills catalog contract chip/review strip backed by /skills/index metadata, facets, reuse groups, and proposal summary fields.
Sixty-fifth guardrail pass adds an IDACC-side Brain Controller Sync card in Identity & Keys backed by read-only /controllers and scoped current-agent matching.
Sixty-sixth guardrail pass adds a guided Settings Local driving path and tightens local readiness to synced/live local backends rather than installed/configured hints.
Sixty-seventh guardrail pass adds guarded IDACC confirmations before opening Brain Health or Learning because those dashboard tabs load report/replay surfaces that are not yet pure passive summaries.

Sixty-eighth guardrail pass adds an IDACC-side Brain Core health chip backed by safe GET /health so Brain liveness, route skew, and sqlite-vec degradation are visible without opening guarded report tabs.

Sixty-ninth guardrail pass adds an IDACC-side Brain Agents authority chip backed by /fleet-report and /controllers so controller fallback, same-name ambiguity, and SLA coverage are visible before opening Brain Agents.
Seventieth guardrail pass tightens Work Goal Autopilot driver changes and Run now with global active-goal previews plus post-confirm rechecks.

Seventy-first guardrail pass locks the HR Manager default leadership backbone to default/lead plus validator-only default/coder and default/researcher, blocks reassignment of those roles, protects unsaved relay-team switches, and disables no-op Team ops buttons.

Seventy-second guardrail pass prevents HR Manager Route from saving the default team or default backbone agents into a fully blocked relay state while preserving permissive, all-team, and selected-team routing options.

Seventy-third guardrail pass brings HR Manager Build into the same contract: default-team builds wire default/lead, cannot stage a blocked default relay, and do not show stale non-lead stars as effective primary.
Seventy-fourth guardrail pass blocks Settings default-backend promotion until the backend is route-ready and aligns the local-model status chips with that same live/synced/preset model-list contract.
Seventy-fifth guardrail pass adds a Brain-side post-confirm stamp to Capabilities Preview & sync Brain so Brain /skills/index changes during review block the additive catalog write.
Seventy-sixth guardrail pass keeps Computer Use Remove from rebuilding a stale same-named replacement by detaching old scoped authority and rebuilding only when the exact agent id still exists.
Seventy-seventh guardrail pass rechecks Health lifecycle rows and Headroom pilot policy after confirmation so reviewed prompts cannot write stale process or routing-policy state.
Seventy-eighth guardrail pass stamps Projects commit/push and pull reviews with git status plus diff state, then blocks if the repository changes after confirmation.
Seventy-ninth guardrail pass rechecks Identity selected-agent, controller-proof, account, and session stamps after confirmation before privileged wallet/account/key writes.
Eightieth guardrail pass removes the Dashboard drawer's duplicate direct project/org sync buttons, leaving observe actions and owner-page handoffs only.
Eighty-first guardrail pass makes Brain /fleet-report prefer the manager /teams inventory over stale BRAIN_FLEET_TEAMS subsets, with strict env override and warnings.
Eighty-second guardrail pass stamps the full Brain Health approval review shape so payload or risk changes during review block queued mutations.
Eighty-third guardrail pass labels Brain Skills execution metrics outside the fetched stat window as not fetched instead of zero.
Eighty-fourth guardrail pass adds a Brain Learning confirmation before the eval replay POST runs and can update replay evidence.
Eighty-fifth guardrail pass makes Brain Agents treat controller-registry fetch failure as unknown, not proof of unlinked agents.
Eighty-sixth guardrail pass keeps Brain Graph filter controls, JSON export, and popout URL parameters synchronized.
Eighty-seventh guardrail pass documents the Brain Fleet env contract so default installs use live all-team manager discovery and strict subsets are explicit.
Eighty-eighth guardrail pass aligns starter-pair and solidity-pair templates with the default primary/secondary validator loop and task-discipline defaults.
Eighty-ninth guardrail pass tightens default/coder and default/researcher self-descriptions as secondary validation leads while keeping team-coordinator on default/lead only.
Ninetieth guardrail pass removes HR Manager duplicate Structure lead writes and Route relay team-reassignment controls so routing and membership edits stay on their owner panes.
Ninety-first guardrail pass makes Settings Local LLM stacks start on starter paths, exposes setup notes, and demotes guided/advanced stack commands to review-first actions.

Ninety-second guardrail pass makes Capabilities Skills a safer Brain-aware catalog by blocking sync without Brain core health, preserving the full Brain index after sync, surfacing catalog/facet counts, and preventing deletion of skills still referenced by fleet agents.
Ninety-third guardrail pass makes Computer Use arming main-process authoritative and clears stale Bless selections across team changes.
Ninety-fourth guardrail pass makes Settings first-run readiness check the manager-side Control Center extension /capabilities contract before the app can present itself as ready.
Ninety-fifth guardrail pass folds the id-agents Control Center manifest into a shared IDACC contract and requires exact feature plus route coverage before Settings marks the manager extension ready.
Ninety-sixth guardrail pass adds a copyable Settings manager compatibility report so stale-manager handoffs stay explicit without running an unapproved manager updater.

Ninety-seventh guardrail pass updates README and product-spec first-run guidance so downloaded installs know to verify the manager-extension contract and no longer see stale Settings coordinator-dropdown documentation.
Ninety-eighth guardrail pass keeps HR Manager Route from assigning non-running agents as current coordinators and makes stale coordinator evidence visible in routing overview and hierarchy.

Ninety-ninth guardrail pass makes Capabilities flag Brain Fleet count drift when Brain /fleet-report disagrees with IDACC's live all-team store, covering stale Brain states such as Brain 19/38 beside IDACC 0/67.

One-hundredth guardrail pass makes the nested Brain /fleet-report mark fallback:ID_TEAM inventory as partial when manager /teams is unavailable, so a one-team fallback cannot masquerade as an all-team live snapshot.
One-hundred-first guardrail pass makes Capabilities Brain dashboard launchers warning-styled and confirmation-gated whenever the target tab already has an active IDACC review state.

One-hundred-second guardrail pass adds nested Brain dashboard nav confirmations before handoffs into Health or Learning, so in-Brain navigation no longer bypasses the non-passive tab warnings after Brain redeploy.

One-hundred-third guardrail pass tightens Work Create Work so live dispatches recheck selected agents/team leads and interval schedule/loop/dream shortcuts disclose heartbeat replacement before writing.
One-hundred-fourth guardrail pass makes Projects workspace sync preview root/folder/add/adopt counts and recheck the scan after confirmation before writing tracker rows.
One-hundred-fifth guardrail pass makes Health Probe all use the all-team running roster it displays, preview and recheck targets, and probe each target in its own team.
One-hundred-sixth guardrail pass makes Identity Create account require controller proof in the renderer and backend before persisting smart-account state.
One-hundred-seventh guardrail pass makes HR Manager Team ops preview and recheck the exact roster before Probe, Start, Stop, or Rebuild touches a team.
One-hundred-eighth guardrail pass makes Capabilities MCP registry removal and library skill deletion use post-confirm and fresh all-team delete checks.
One-hundred-ninth guardrail pass makes Computer Use attach/detach exact-row only and stamps armed-list refreshes before broker blessed-set rewrites.
One-hundred-tenth guardrail pass makes Settings provider Connect & sync stamp-check the provider row in the bridge before model-list metadata writes.
One-hundred-eleventh guardrail pass makes Brain dashboard HTML and JSON responses opt out of browser caching so stale Fleet/Health/Skills/Learning/Agents/Graph state cannot look current.
One-hundred-twelfth guardrail pass folds that cache contract into IDACC's Brain client and Capabilities review chips.
One-hundred-thirteenth guardrail pass makes org-sync instruction authority team-scoped and backend-enforces the default/lead plus default-validator backbone.
One-hundred-fourteenth guardrail pass makes Settings local-stack install state exact, so same-port overlap no longer masquerades as an installed stack.
One-hundred-fifteenth guardrail pass makes HR Route display the effective default/lead primary and removes the stale disabled non-default primary button.
One-hundred-sixteenth guardrail pass makes Settings local image generator Clear write an explicit null server instead of reusing stale input state.
One-hundred-seventeenth guardrail pass makes the Tauri adapter preserve validated team scope for Capabilities skill, MCP, and rebuild actions.
One-hundred-eighteenth guardrail pass makes Computer Use fail closed in non-Electron shells with explicit unavailable status instead of crashy or half-live controls.
One-hundred-nineteenth guardrail pass makes the Brain Fleet dashboard show cache fallback as cached records instead of a live running count.
One-hundred-twentieth guardrail pass makes Brain Skills label IDACC counts as graph nodes and hand live local-catalog truth back to IDACC Capabilities.
One-hundred-twenty-first guardrail pass labels Brain Graph refresh/export controls as snapshot actions and warns when an opened graph snapshot ages before routing, identity, or lifecycle decisions.
One-hundred-twenty-second guardrail pass brings workspace agent instruction sources back in line with team-scoped default leadership and adds structured worker completion packets.
One-hundred-twenty-third guardrail pass makes Work Goal Autopilot re-read active goals around Brain sync and before task creation, so automatic goal add/subtract changes cannot spawn stale work.
One-hundred-twenty-fourth guardrail pass makes Settings local discovery re-probe before backend writes and removes inferred stack uninstall actions from configured/running snapshot evidence.
The guardrail passes favor confirmation prompts and explicit disclosures over removing controls; the open UX decision is whether to replace confirmed shortcuts with preview-and-apply flows.
Most important simplification: separate status/observation pages from mutation/control surfaces wherever possible.
pages.body:
# Sync & Guardrails Audit

This page records the ongoing pass over Dashboard, Work, Projects, Health, Identity & Keys, HR Manager, Capabilities, Computer Use, Settings, and the Brain dashboard tabs. It now documents one hundred twenty-four conservative guardrail implementation passes.

## Current build state

IDACC is sync-bus aware across dashboard, work, projects, health, plans, goals, loops, schedules, dreams, agent capability changes, and org sync. The guardrail layer favors fresh reads, preview prompts, scoped team/name authority, owner-page handoffs, locked default leadership roles, team-scoped org-instruction authority, effective-primary HR routing, route-ready backend defaults, bridge-stamped provider sync writes, explicit local image-server clear writes, Tauri/Electron team-scoped Capabilities actions, Computer Use unavailable-state guardrails outside Electron, manager-extension feature and route compatibility checks with a copyable report, first-run docs that tell operators to verify that contract, HR routing that labels stale/non-running coordinators and only promotes running agents, two-sided Brain skill sync stamps, main-process-authoritative Computer Use arming, exact-row Computer Use attach/detach, stamped armed-list refreshes, exact-agent Computer Use rebuilds, Health all-team probe previews, post-confirm Health lifecycle/pilot checks, post-confirm Work create-dispatch checks, Work Goal Autopilot background rechecks, Projects workspace-sync previews, post-confirm Projects git/diff stamps, post-confirm Identity account/session stamps, Identity account-creation proof gates, HR Manager exact-roster Team ops, Brain Fleet team-list authority, Brain Fleet cached-record display guardrails, IDACC-side Brain Fleet count-drift review, Brain fallback-team partial authority, review-aware Brain dashboard launchers, Brain-side Health/Learning nav confirmations, Brain dashboard no-store cache headers, IDACC-verified Brain cache headers, documented Brain Fleet env defaults, full Brain Health approval stamps, Brain Skills stat-boundary labels, Brain Skills graph-vs-local catalog labels, confirmed Brain Learning replay, unknown-safe Brain Agents controller evidence, synced Brain Graph filter/export/popout links plus snapshot-age warnings, workspace agent instruction parity, worker completion-packet discipline, preloaded starter/onchain coordination templates, default-team secondary-validator self-descriptions, HR Manager owner-pane routing cleanup, starter-first local stack setup, exact local-stack install identity, Brain-aware skill catalog summary/delete guardrails, Capabilities catalog-delete rechecks, Settings local-discovery re-probe writes, stack catalog install-state truth, and an observe-only Dashboard drawer before mutation.

The latest pass tightens Settings local model setup. Local discovery Add/Add all now refresh and compare server snapshots before backend writes, and Local LLM stack rows no longer offer uninstall commands from configured-backend or last-scan-running evidence.

## Boundaries

Dashboard stays observe, talk, and refresh. Work owns plans, goals, tasks, loops, schedules, dreams, and guarded Autopilot driver actions. HR Manager owns hierarchy, relay policy, team lifecycle, and org sync. Capabilities owns MCP, skills, plugins, additive Brain catalog sync, read-only Brain contract chips, safe Brain Core health, Brain Agents authority review, Brain Fleet count-drift and partial-authority review, exact team-scoped capability writes, in-use skill delete lockouts, and guarded Brain dashboard handoffs. Identity owns local controller proof, scoped key actions, legacy authority review, and read-only Brain /controllers comparison. Computer Use owns the Electron local broker, blessed-agent attachment, approval flow, immediate stop controls, and unavailable-state disclosure in shells without the native broker. Settings owns local model/backend setup, local image generator routing, and read-only manager-extension compatibility checks, but keeps coordinator/routing edits handed to HR Manager; local discovery rows are snapshots and stack configured/running badges are not package install authority. Brain Fleet, Health, Skills, Learning, Agents, and Graph remain status surfaces; cached Brain Fleet rows and additive Brain skill graph nodes are diagnostic records, not live lifecycle or local-catalog authority, unless a future write flow receives its own guarded preview-and-apply path.

## Open decisions

Decide whether to add pure-read Brain Health/Learning summary endpoints, or keep the guarded IDACC confirmations while report routes continue to reconcile stale eval fixtures. Decide separately whether IDACC should remain pure-client with guided manager instructions, open a reviewed Terminal manager updater, or bundle a compatible manager.

# Wiki

Wiki is both the human documentation page and the editable schematic for the Control Center UI. Each article explains the operator purpose of a page; the structured sections below preserve tabs, tiles, fields, controls, displays, routes, and source files for implementation detail.

## Use it for

- Search pages, controls, routes, source files, and notes from inside the desktop app.
- Confirm how a UI surface is intended to behave before editing code or filing follow-up work.
- Keep app navigation metadata and human documentation in one reviewed JSON artifact.

## Editing rule

The desktop app hot-reload polls CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json through the main-process wiki bridge. Sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility still come from pages[].nav for implemented routes, so documentation edits must preserve that metadata and keep every page.body non-empty.
